Anti-aphrodisiac: A substance capable of blunting the venereal appetite
Anti-bilious: Opposed to biliousness, acting on the bile
Anthilitic: Preventing the formation of calculi in the urinary organs
Anti-syphilitic: Opposed to or curing venereal diseases
Anti-venomous: Used against bites of insects, snakes, etc.
Counter-irritant: Causing irritation in one part to relieve pain in another part
Escharotic: A substance which, applied to a living part, causes an eschar
Female complaints: Ailments peculiar to women, as dismenorrhœa, amenorrhœa, etc.
Abortive: A medicine which claims the property of causing abortion
Acidulous: Substances which possess a sourish taste
Alexipharmic: Preventing the bad effects of poisons inwardly
Alterative: Producing a salutary change without perceptible evacuation
Anodyne: Relieving pain, or causing it to cease
Aphrodisiac: Medicine believed capable of exciting the venereal appetite
Astringent: Having the property of constringing the organic texture
Balsamic: Healing or soothing to inflamed parts
Caustic: The property of burning or disorganizing animal substance
Cephalic: Relating to diseases of the head
Condiment: Improving the saver of food, as salt, pepper, salad, etc.
Cordial: A worm stomachic, exciting the heart
Deobstruent: Removing obstructions, aperient in a general sense
Dessicative: Drying the moisture of wounds and ulcers
Detergent: Cleansing to wounds, boils, or ulcers
Diuretic: Increasing the secretion and flow of urine
Exanthematous: Relating to eruption or skin diseases
Expectorant: A medicine capable of facilitating expectoration
Farinaceous: Containing farina, mealy, employed as nutriment
Forage: Used as food for domestic animals
Fumigating: Disinfecting by burning substances which counteract obnoxious odors
Hepatic: Relating to diseases of the liver
Herpatic: Relating to or curing eruptions or skin diseases, as ringworm
Hydragogue: Medicines that cause watery evacuations and believed capable of expelling serum
Laxative: A medicine that acts gently on the bowels, without griping
Lithontryptic: Medicine believed to dissolve calculi in the urinary organs
Maturating: Favoring the maturation or ripening of tumors, boils, and ulcers
Nephreticum: Relating to or curing kidney complaints
Nervine: Allaying nervous excitement, acting on the nervous system
Nutritious: Having the quality of nourishing or sustaining life
Opthalmicum: A remedy for diseases of the eye
Parturient: A medicine that induces or promotes labor or child-birth
Pectoral: Medicines considered proper for relieving afflictions of the chest
Poison: Producing death if taken in improper doses
Pungent: Biting, hot, acrid, prickly to the taste
Purgative: A medicine that physics more powerfully than a cathartic
Refrigerant: Depressing the morbid temperature of the body, cooling
Salad: Fresh herbs eaten as condiments or as food
Saponaceous: Soapy, making a lather with water
Sedative: Directly depressing to the vital forces
Stimulant: Exciting or inducing organic action of the animal economy
Stomachic: Strengthening and giving tone to the stomach, tonic
Styptic: Externally astringent, arresting hemorrhage or bleeding
Tonic: Permanently strengthening, in a durable manner invigorating
Viscid: Having a glutinous or ropy consistency, tenacious
Vulnerary: Healing to fresh cuts or wounds
